title: Jovan Damjanović - Wikipedia
body: Serbian footballer Jovan Damjanović Personal information Full name Jovan Damjanović Date of birth ( 1982-10-04 ) 4 October 1982 (age 43) Place of birth Knin , SR Croatia , SFR Yugoslavia Height 1.93 m (6 ft 4 in) Position Striker Team information Current team OFK Beograd (manager) Youth career Red Star Belgrade Senior career* Years Team Apps ( Gls ) 2000–2001 Sutjeska Nikšić 5 (0) 2001–2002 Radnički Obrenovac 15 (1) 2002–2003 Rad 34 (4) 2004 Železnik 14 (1) 2005–2006 Borac Čačak 37 (14) 2006–2007 Ried 36 (9) 2007–2009 SC Paderborn 07 31 (8) 2009–2010 Wehen Wiesbaden 23 (1) 2011–2012 Borac Čačak 29 (8) 2012–2013 Dinamo Minsk 25 (6) 2013 → Dinamo Brest (loan) 4 (0) 2013 OFK Beograd 9 (4) 2014 Hunan Billows 17 (8) 2015 Novi Pazar 11 (0) 2016 Voždovac 9 (1) Total 299 (65) International career 1998 FR Yugoslavia U16 3 (0) 2000–2001 FR Yugoslavia U18 8 (5) 2011 Serbia 3 (0) Managerial career 2018 Voždovac 2020–2021 Voždovac 2021–2023 Serbia U18 2023–2024 Serbia U17 2024–2025 TSC 2026– OFK Beograd * Club domestic league appearances and goals Jovan Damjanović ( Serbian Cyrillic : Јован Дамјановић; born 4 October 1982) is a Serbian retired footballer who played as a striker . He is currently the head coach of OFK Beograd . International career [ edit ] In 2011, Damjanović was capped three times for Serbia . [ 1 ] He previously represented FR Yugoslavia at the 2001 UEFA European Under-18 Championship , scoring three goals in the process, as the team lost in the third-place match. Personal life [ edit ] Jovan is father of the serbian football player Aleksa Damjanović .
